creator: keith.gaughan@gmail.com
text:
On 02/04/2008, Michael Johnson <ahze@freebsd.org> wrote:

> I'm using the hda sound driver and I don't have this problem, can you send
> us the output of "pkg_info -E gst\*" and your CFLAGS?

Certainly. Here's the package list:

gstreamer-0.10.18
gstreamer-ffmpeg-0.10.3
gstreamer-plugins-0.10.18,3
gstreamer-plugins-a52dec-0.10.7_2,3
gstreamer-plugins-bad-0.10.6_2,3
gstreamer-plugins-cdparanoia-0.10.18,3
gstreamer-plugins-core-0.10_9
gstreamer-plugins-dts-0.10.6_2,3
gstreamer-plugins-dvd-0.10.7_2,3
gstreamer-plugins-esound-0.10.7_2,3
gstreamer-plugins-gconf-0.10.7_2,3
gstreamer-plugins-gnomevfs-0.10.18,3
gstreamer-plugins-good-0.10.7_2,3
gstreamer-plugins-hal-0.10.7_2,3
gstreamer-plugins-libpng-0.10.7_2,3
gstreamer-plugins-mad-0.10.7_2,3
gstreamer-plugins-mp3-0.10.0
gstreamer-plugins-mpeg2dec-0.10.7_2,3
gstreamer-plugins-ogg-0.10.18,3
gstreamer-plugins-pango-0.10.18,3
gstreamer-plugins-theora-0.10.18,3
gstreamer-plugins-ugly-0.10.7_3,3
gstreamer-plugins-vorbis-0.10.18,3
gstreamer-plugins-xvid-0.10.6_2,3

I'm just using the regular CFLAGS: -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe,
though the WITH_OPTIMIZED_CFLAGS knob is set for any ports that'll use
it.

--
